evals.prompt: Our Angular prerender app on Azure App Service keeps calling the old API host after I change API_URL in App Settings. Diagnose and fix it in code, then tell me what to verify in Azure.
evals.expected_output: Implements runtime injection that updates apiUrl on each startup, addresses runtime-config caching risk, and provides concrete Azure validation steps.
expectations: Identifies runtime-config.js + server startup injection as the runtime boundary
Fix avoids placeholder-only replacement and updates current apiUrl value
Provides Azure checks for startup command, env var key, and restart behavior

evals.prompt: I need to support API_URL, AUTH_URL, and FEATURE_FLAG_X in Angular without rebuilding between environments. Implement a generic runtime env var pattern and document it.
evals.expected_output: Provides a scalable runtime config mapping pattern for multiple variables, wires Angular consumption safely, and updates docs to match real behavior.
expectations: Design supports multiple env vars via runtime config object
Frontend reads values from runtime global object
Server-side injection maps process.env values into runtime config
Documentation reflects runtime (not build-time-only) variable flow

evals.prompt: Given this startup command fallback chain (wwwroot -> drop-staging -> drop-production), ensure the app always injects env vars into the actual served runtime-config.js and explain how to confirm in Kudu logs.
evals.expected_output: Handles path resolution and startup fallback concerns, ensures injection targets served path, and gives practical Kudu log verification guidance.
expectations: Addresses resolved dist path and not just assumed default path
Includes actionable log statements for resolved path and injection status
Explains how startup fallback can hide wrong server.js being used
Provides Kudu log checks tied to actual runtime-config target
